definition: The $900B-$1.1T maturity wall (2025-2027) is the bridge mechanism converting fiscal dynamics to credit outcomes. At true leverage of 7.5-8.5x (addback-adjusted), debt service at 500bp+ OAS consumes 70-80% of actual cash EBITDA. Severity is GDP-dependent: manageable at consensus 2.0-2.5% growth (Kalecki sustained), solvency wall at growth below 1.0%.
evidence: Maturities are contractual facts; coupon step-ups (300-500bp) are arithmetic
Prior maturity walls (2012, 2014, 2019) defused by amend-and-extend under ZIRP conditions no longer available at SOFR ~4.3%
Calendar-identifiable catalysts more analytically useful than probabilistic supply shock scenarios (pair_1)
At true leverage of 7.5-8.5x, debt service at 500bp+ OAS consumes 70-80% of cash EBITDA
open_questions: What proportion of the maturity wall can be extended under current rate conditions?
At what GDP growth rate does the maturity wall become a solvency wall?
How does the Kalecki profit floor interact with individual firm leverage dynamics?
